text:
SpaceX Shows $4.3 Billion Loss While Elon Musk Targets Record $75 Billion IPO — Net Loss of $4.28 Billion on Revenue of $4.69 Billion in Q1 2026; To Eclipse Saudi Aramco's $29.4 Billion IPO Record

The IPO — Historic Scale
SpaceX filed publicly for what stands to be the largest-ever initial public offering

Targeting as much as $75 billion in its listing at a valuation of more than $2 trillion

Would eclipse the previous IPO record of $29.4 billion set by Saudi Aramco in 2019

Formal marketing expected to begin as early as June 4

Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley leading the IPO; Citigroup, JPMorgan Chase and 18 other banks also working on the deal

The Financials — Losses Despite Scale
Net loss of $4.28 billion on revenue of $4.69 billion in Q1 2026

Compared with a net loss of $528 million on revenue of ~$4 billion a year earlier — losses widened dramatically

Filing made with the US Securities and Exchange Commission

The Control Structure — Musk Stays in Charge
Musk currently owns 12.3% of Class A shares and 93.6% of Class B shares

Gives him 85.1% of the voting power in the company

Class B shares carry 10 votes each — Musk will continue to control the company after the IPO

Why This IPO Matters — Broader Impact
Audacious plan by Musk for an IPO of unprecedented size set to transform both public and private markets if it succeeds

A blockbuster listing and rising share price after would help dispel concern over whether private companies with limited financial disclosures are reaching unjustified valuations in venture capital-led funding rounds

SpaceX's debut would open the door for other private giants to plan their own mega-IPOs

The Domino Effect — Who's Next
OpenAI and Anthropic PBC — whose products capture greater chatbot traffic than SpaceX's Grok per Similarweb — preparing for listings as soon as this year

SpaceX IPO seen as potential catalyst for a wave of mega tech listings

Core Theme 
SpaceX's filing for a potentially $75 billion IPO — the largest in history — is a watershed moment for global capital markets; despite posting a $4.28 billion loss in Q1 2026, Musk is betting that SpaceX's scale, strategic importance and brand power will command a $2 trillion valuation, while a super-voting share structure ensures he retains iron control post-listing — and if it succeeds, it could trigger a historic wave of mega-IPOs from OpenAI, Anthropic and other private giants.

• 📈 Ruby-Fill® recorded strong 35% growth in FY26; PET facility investments remain on track

• 🤧 Allergy Immunotherapy business focused on strengthening competitive position & new product development; gained market share in FY26

• 💉 CDMO Sterile Injectables segment aims to double Spokane, US capacity; Line-3 expected to achieve peak revenue by FY28 ahead of plans

• 🧪 CRDMO business focused on adding large pharma customers & scaling API custom manufacturing; revenue mix improving

• 💊 Generics business plans new US launches and expansion in profitable non-US international markets

• 🆕 Company launched 4 new products during FY26

text:
Vinay Dube, Founder & CEO Akasa Air: 'West Asian Disruption Impact Limited to 15% of Operations'; Airline Delivers 40%+ Top-Line Growth in FY26 — 226 Aircraft Order Book, Vietnam Entry & Long-Term Bullish Stance

FY26 Performance — A Strong Year Despite Headwinds
Delivered over 40% top-line growth despite challenges including Pehelgam terror attack, Operation Sindoor and Air India tragedy

Ebitda margins and unit revenues improved significantly

Reliability metrics including on-time performance, cancellations and baggage delivery remained strong

Akasa has now flown over 27 million passengers and more than 11,000 pets since launch

Fleet & Network — Where Akasa Stands Today
Currently operates 38 aircraft with an order book of 226 aircraft

Serves 34 destinations — 27 domestic and 7 international cities

Last year added Phuket and expanded Jeddah operations; this year announced Vietnam entry

West Asia Conflict — Limited But Real Impact
Before conflict, operated to Kuwait, Doha, Abu Dhabi, Riyadh and Jeddah

Nearly 80-85% of West Asian operations concentrated in Abu Dhabi and Jeddah — both least affected

Currently operating full schedules to Abu Dhabi and Jeddah

Riyadh, Kuwait and Doha remain suspended — but these accounted for only ~15% of West Asian operations

Suspended routes account for only ~14 weekly frequencies out of more than 1,000 weekly frequencies operated by Akasa

Capacity & Scheduling — No Cuts Made
No flight reductions made despite rising fuel costs and geopolitical uncertainty

Operating planned schedules other than temporary suspensions to Riyadh, Doha and Kuwait

Overall schedule significantly larger than last year due to fleet growth

FY27 Outlook — Cautiously Optimistic
Demand in April and May has remained healthy

Too early to make full-year projections given ongoing Iran conflict and broader geopolitical uncertainties

Outlook will depend on how long the situation persists

Core Theme 
Akasa Air is demonstrating remarkable resilience — delivering 40%+ top-line growth in a year marked by terror attacks, military operations and regional conflict, while keeping West Asian disruption contained to just 15% of its operations; with a 226-aircraft order book, Vietnam on the horizon and domestic demand holding firm, the airline remains firmly on its long-term growth trajectory even as geopolitical clouds linger overhead.

Life Insurance Corp: Guidance -  - Concall Update

Management achieved its long-held ambition to cross INR 5,000 crore in premium income through Bancassurance and Alternate Channels in FY26.

Management expects persistency to improve across all cohorts in the future, driven by the increased ticket size of policies, with the full effect of changes from October 2024 yet to unfold.

The company anticipates significantly high maturity claims in FY26 and FY27, particularly from a cohort of Jeevan Jyoti policies with high sum assured that were sold 25 years ago.

The dividend payout ratio will depend on future regulatory changes, specifically the planned introduction of risk-based capital, which requires building reserves to maintain a comfortable solvency level.

Management expects VNB margins to further improve, with a goal to cross 20%, but acknowledges that the journey will be gradual and dynamic, focusing on VNB growth through improved margins or business growth.

Interesting Chart - Capex Required to Integrate in Solar Chain goes up significanlty

Waaree’s biggest advantage in this solar cycle may not just be demand.

It is the CAPEX barrier created by deeper backward integration.

The numbers explain it clearly:

Module-only capacity needs ~₹100–150 Cr/GW.
Cell + module integration needs ~₹500–600 Cr/GW.
Ingot + wafer + cell + module needs ~₹1,100–1,200 Cr/GW.
Full poly-to-module integration needs ~₹1,500–1,600 Cr/GW.

That is nearly a 10–17x jump in capital intensity as the value chain goes deeper.
This changes the competitive landscape.
On the supply side, fewer players can afford this scale, capital, technology, and execution capability.
On the demand side, ALMM and domestic sourcing requirements can further shift opportunities toward integrated domestic players.

So Waaree gets a dual-cycle benefit:

Competition reduces as integration becomes capital-heavy

Demand expands for approved, integrated domestic manufacturers

This is why Waaree is not just participating in the solar upcycle.

It is structurally positioned to remain one of the front-runners.

So what everybody sees as an oversupply or competitive issue may not be true

Kalyan Capitals Ltd (KCL) – A Quiet Turnaround Story? 

What does the company do?

KCL is registered as a Non-Systemically Important Non-Deposit Taking Base Layer NBFC-ICC.

The company primarily provides funded facilities to individuals and corporates. Now, it has also entered the gold loan business — a segment seeing strong traction across India due to rising gold prices and demand for secured retail lending.

Business Momentum Improving
FY25 loan disbursement stood at ₹203.2 Cr, up ~24% YoY
Net NPA ratio remained around 3%

Market Cap: ₹48.4 Cr
Enterprise Value: ₹304 Cr
P/B Ratio: 0.92x
Stock P/E: 12.6x
EV/EBITDA: 10.2x
Current Price: ₹9.21
Book Value: ₹10

The stock currently trades below book value and at a valuation lower than many listed NBFC peers.

Growth Metrics

3-Year Revenue CAGR: 40%
TTM Growth: 25%

That kind of growth with improving operations often becomes the start of a turnaround rerating story.

text:
Talbros Automotive Components: Guidance -  - Concall Update

Management expects to deliver 15% to 20% year-on-year revenue growth in FY27, targeting to cross INR 1,000 crores in top line for the year, while maintaining EBITDA margins between 17% to 18%.

The company plans a CapEx of INR 51 crores in FY26 and INR 103 crores in FY27, with specific allocations for the current year including INR 16 crores for the Gasket division, INR 60 crores for the Forging division, INR 20 crores for Marelli Talbros, and INR 7 crores for Talbros Marugo.

Joint ventures are projected for significant growth in the coming year, with Marelli Talbros Chassis Systems expected to grow between 35% to 40% and Talbros Marugo around 15% in top line.

Standalone businesses, Gasket and Forging, are expected to achieve double-digit top-line growth in the current year, driven by INR 50 to INR 70 crores in new billing from new part numbers, with forging commercialization starting in October.

Management anticipates the second half of FY27 to be significantly better than the first half, with Q1 FY27's top line expected to be in line with Q4 FY26.

@Concalls3

Amagi Media Labs: Guidance - - Concall Update

Management did not provide prescriptive quantitative guidance for FY27 revenue growth or margin expansion, but outlined focus areas and aspirations.

For FY27, the company will focus on durable revenue growth, operating leverage, and cash conversion.

The aspiration is to continue the trajectory of the last three years, balancing top-line growth with fiscal responsibility.

For Q1, based on FY25 and FY26 averages, revenue represented about 22% of the full year and adjusted EBITDA about 11%.

Current PAT margin levels are not considered steady state, with headroom and upside expected, and the company plans "ahead of the curve investments" in AI.

@Concalls3

Bosch: Guidance -  - Concall Update

Bosch Limited's outlook for fiscal year 2027 is one of "cautious optimism," anticipating a "flattish" overall growth due to potential headwinds like the West Asia crisis impacting crude oil prices, but confident in the government's capital expenditure to bolster domestic demand.

The Indian automotive industry is expected to see steady growth in FY '27, with specific segment projections: Passenger Vehicles (steady growth, but Q1 demand may be affected by geopolitical uncertainties), Commercial Vehicles (stable growth), and Tractors (stable growth, with El Nino as a downside risk).

The three-wheeler segment is likely to experience positive growth, while the two-wheeler segment expects healthy growth, though a below-normal monsoon could weigh on rural demand.

Content per vehicle is expected to constantly increase in the upcoming fiscal year, particularly with the upcoming CAFE Phase 3 regulations scheduled for April 2027, which will meaningfully improve content per vehicle.

The joint venture with Brakes India Private Limited and Wheels India Limited for advanced air systems is expected to commence operations by the end of 2026, with samples in 2027 and series readiness by 2028.

ICICI Sec on Bosch
Upgrade to Buy from Add; Hike TP to Rs 44000 from Rs 40000

Parent company's focus on the India business has sharpened in the recent past

Entry into braking and safety components could help expand its product portfolio and drive content-led growth

Regulatory changes, including upcoming CAFÉ/BS-VII norms, could further support its content growth journey

Expect a 23%/30% revenue/EBITDA CAGR over FY26-28

Kalyan Capitals Ltd (KCL) – A Quiet Turnaround Story? 

What does the company do?

KCL is registered as a Non-Systemically Important Non-Deposit Taking Base Layer NBFC-ICC.

The company primarily provides funded facilities to individuals and corporates. Now, it has also entered the gold loan business — a segment seeing strong traction across India due to rising gold prices and demand for secured retail lending.

Business Momentum Improving
FY25 loan disbursement stood at ₹203.2 Cr, up ~24% YoY
Net NPA ratio remained around 3%

Valuation Looks Interesting Despite improvement in business activity:

Market Cap: ₹48.4 Cr
Enterprise Value: ₹304 Cr
P/B Ratio: 0.92x
Stock P/E: 12.6x
EV/EBITDA: 10.2x
Current Price: ₹9.21
Book Value: ₹10

The stock currently trades below book value and at a valuation lower than many listed NBFC peers.

Growth Metrics
5-Year Revenue CAGR: 39%
3-Year Revenue CAGR: 40%
TTM Growth: 25%

That kind of growth with improving operations often becomes the start of a turnaround rerating story.

text:
Kalyan Capitals Ltd (KCL) – A Quiet Turnaround Story? 

What does the company do?

KCL is registered as a Non-Systemically Important Non-Deposit Taking Base Layer NBFC-ICC.

The company primarily provides funded facilities to individuals and corporates. Now, it has also entered the gold loan business — a segment seeing strong traction across India due to rising gold prices and demand for secured retail lending.

Business Momentum Improving
FY25 loan disbursement stood at ₹203.2 Cr, up ~24% YoY
Net NPA ratio remained around 3%

Valuation Looks Interesting Despite improvement in business activity:

Market Cap: ₹48.4 Cr
Enterprise Value: ₹304 Cr
P/B Ratio: 0.92x
Stock P/E: 12.6x
EV/EBITDA: 10.2x
Current Price: ₹9.21
Book Value: ₹10

The stock currently trades below book value and at a valuation lower than many listed NBFC peers.

Growth Metrics
5-Year Revenue CAGR: 39%
3-Year Revenue CAGR: 40%
TTM Growth: 25%

That kind of growth with improving operations often becomes the start of a turnaround rerating story.

*CLSA on ITC*
O-P, TP Rs 394
Cigarettes Ebit grew 7.2% YoY in 4QFY26 and was 15% ahead of consensus. The quarter was marked by a significant increase in cigarette taxes, which likely led to channel filling and potential product mix changes due to pricing differentials, thereby impacting overall performance during the period. Cigarettes net revenue for the year grew 8.2% YoY. FMCG revenue other than cigarettes grew 15% YoY and was 2%/5% above our estimate/consensus.

*CITI on ITC*
Sell, TP Rs 290
4Q performance is not directly comparable with prior periods given mid-quarter changes in cigarette taxation
Estimate ~7% volume decline, driving the 24% drop in cigarette net revenues, reflecting: 1) strong January demand; 2) trade inventory stocking ahead of price hikes; and 3) demand slowdown thereafter following staggered price increases to offset higher taxes.
Reported cigarette EBIT growth of ~7% is also not comparable, as it appears supported by one-off inventory gains on pre-tax revision stock
Importantly, key structural uncertainties persist, particularly around price elasticity, demand sensitivity to successive price hikes, and potential downtrading to illicit products

*Jefferies on ITC*
Hold, TP Rs 350

However, real test is in 1QFY27, when impact of sharp tax hike will be visible, as price hikes are only partial while volumes & margins will be affected.

*CITI on LIC*
Buy,  TP Rs 1475

CLSA on Honasa

O-P, TP Rs 434

reported revenue growth of 23% YoY (28% growth ex-change in accounting) 4%/6% ahead of our estimate/consensus. 

Volume grew 30% while its margin expanded more than 650bps YoY, leading to a beat on Ebitda of 19%/24% versus our estimate/consensus. 

Three things stood out for us. 

Fisrst, Mamaearth grew in the teens YoY and management expects double-digit growth to continue. 

Second, offtake growth in general trade and modern trade was up 30% YoY, a healthy sign of rising brand traction. 

Third, operating leverage, albeit on a soft base, helped Ebitda beat our estimate by more than 140bps. 

We think Honasa’s runway to scale brands via its focus categories and hero SKUs remains long. 

Execution of the Mamaearth turnaround continues to improve and we expect margin expansion to continue.

We expect Ebitda margin expansion to continue, driven by rising salience of own brands and operating leverage.

Nifty 50 wiped out all its opening gains and formed a long red candle on the daily timeframe, indicating pressure at higher levels, although the higher lows formation continued. It failed to sustain above short-term moving averages on a closing basis and, as a result, remained below all key moving averages (10, 20, 50, 100, and 200-day EMAs). The index also stayed below the 38.2 percent Fibonacci retracement level of the April rally, as well as the correction from the February  high to the April low. RSI, at 45.55, remained sideways, while the MACD stayed below the zero and signal lines, though the red histogram bars continued to shrink for the sixth consecutive session. All this indicates continued weakness, although the reduction in histogram bars suggests that bearish momentum may be easing gradually.

The price action in the Nifty Bank was also similar to the Nifty 50, as it formed a bearish candle with minor upper and lower shadows on the daily charts, indicating profit booking at higher levels. The index could not surpass the 50 percent Fibonacci retracement level of the April rally on a closing basis, while continuing to trade below all key moving averages. RSI, at 40.53, remained sideways, while the MACD maintained a sell crossover. However, the red histogram bars have been shrinking for the fourth straight session. All this indicates that the bearish sentiment persists, though selling pressure appears to be gradually weakening.

Stocks To Watch

ITC Ltd reported 5% YoY increase in consolidated net profit for Q4 at ₹5,113 crore. 

LIC reported a 23% YoY rise in consolidated net profit for Q4, supported by improved operating performance and strong growth across key business metrics.

Gail India reported a 21.2% QoQ decline in net profit for Q4 at ₹1,262.2 crore. 

Max Healthcare Institute Ltd reported a 7.3% YoY increase in consolidated net profit for Q4 at ₹342.2 crore. 

One 97 Communications Ltd (PAYTM) to remain in focus on reports that SAIF Partners is looking to pare its stake in the fintech firm through a block deal - SAIF Partners looking to sell 86 lakh shares (1.3 percent equity stake) via block deals, with the floor price set at Rs 1,120.65 per share. 

Tata Steel : Supreme Court has granted a stay on further proceedings in a GST input tax credit (ITC) dispute involving a tax demand of ₹890.52 crore for the period FY19 to FY21.

Aurobindo Pharma reported a 2% YoY increase in consolidated net profit to ₹921 crore for Q4.

FSN E-Commerce Ventures Ltd (Nykaa) reported a sharp rise in Q4 earnings, with consolidated net profit jumping to ₹78.4 crore from ₹20.3 crore YoY, supported by strong revenue growth and margin expansion.

Sun TV Network Ltd reported a subdued set of Q4 earnings, with profit falling sharply amid weaker revenue growth, softer margins and the impact of certain one-off items during the quarter.

RCF Ltd reported a strong set of Q4 earnings, with profit more than doubling on the back of robust revenue growth and improved operating performance.

VA Tech Wabag Ltd reported a 29% YoY increase in standalone net profit to ₹128.3 crore.

Honasa Consumer Ltd reported  176% YoY  rise in net profit for Q4 to ₹69 crore. Annual PAT for FY26 stood at ₹200 crore.

LG Electronics India reported its highest quarterly revenue in Q4, supported by broad-based demand recovery and strong momentum in premium product categories such as large-panel televisions, French-door refrigerators, fully automatic washing machines, and 5-star air conditioners.

Maruti Suzuki India : In view of the sustained increase in input costs, the company has decided to increase the prices of its models across the portfolio by up to Rs 30,000, effective June 2026.

With inflationary pressures remaining elevated and the adverse cost environment persisting, the company has decided to pass on a portion of the increased costs to the market.

Indian Overseas Bank board has approved raising funds of up to Rs 5,000 crore through an FPO, rights issue, QIP, or preferential issue in one or more tranches.
The Board also approved the issuance of 10 crore shares to employees and the issue of BASEL III-compliant Tier II bonds up to a maximum extent of Rs 1,000 crore, with or without a green shoe option.

Fino Payments Bank board is of the view that Rishi Gupta is ‘fit and proper’ to continue as the MD & CEO of the bank. However, Rishi Gupta has opted for voluntary early retirement from the position of MD & CEO, effective May 21.

Meanwhile, the Board approved the extension of Ketan Merchant’s tenure as Interim CEO of the bank, subject to approval from the RBI, for carrying out the duties of MD & CEO for three months effective May 27.

The Board also approved the extension of Anup Agarwal’s tenure as Interim CFO of the bank for three months, effective July 6.

Tata Capital has successfully allotted NCDs worth Rs 2,950 crore on a private placement basis.

Adani Ports completed the acquisition of Jaypee Fertilisers and announced that its arm has incorporated a new wholly-owned subsidiary, Astro Ship Management Angola.

H.G. Infra Engineering board meeting, originally scheduled for May 22, has been officially postponed to a later date.

Kirloskar Electric: The NCLT has formally approved the merger scheme allowing 4 of its subsidiaries to merge into the company.

Eicher Motors plans to acquire a 50% stake in Volvo Financial Services via a Rs 750 crore investment, effectively forming a 50:50 Joint Venture.

text:
Laurus Labs has been officially included in the prestigious Dow Jones Best-in-Class Indices.

Vedanta: The Supreme Court passed a judgment against its unit, Talwandi Sabo Power, upholding an alleged penalty and ordering the company to pay Rs 127 crore to the Punjab State Power Corp.

RailTel Corp confirmed the termination of an Rs 26.7 crore order, citing that it could not execute the work due to a steep price increase in OFC and HDPE pipes caused by the ongoing war crisis.

Union Bank board will meet on May 26 to mull fundraising options via a QIP, rights issue, or other instruments.

TVS Supply Chain's subsidiary, FIT 3PL Warehousing, has allotted shares worth Rs 59.6 crore to the parent company.

Godawari Power has sold a 9.9% stake in Ardent Steel for a total consideration of Rs 22.2 crore.

Varun Beverages has inked a revised Exclusive Bottling Appointment (EBA) pact with PepsiCo India, extending the agreement by 10 years until April 2049. The revised pact removes restrictions, meaning Varun Beverages is no longer bound to act solely as an SPV for PepsiCo.

Kesar India approved the allotment of 71,428 shares upon the conversion of warrants. Additionally, its arm Kesar Infraventures secured an EPC order worth approximately Rs 160 crore from Zee Enterprises for a residential development in Mumbai.

Welspun Corp successfully transferred a 100% stake in Welspun Pipes Company from Welspun Mauritius Holdings to Welspun Global Holdings.

Cholamandalam, the NBFC has allotted NCDs worth Rs 1,000 crore, a figure that includes a greenshoe option of Rs 635 crore.

Man Industries subsidiary acquired a 100% stake in National Pipe Co in Saudi Arabia, adding 4.3 lakh MTPA of pipe manufacturing capacity.

Ola Electric: In a letter to shareholders, the Chairman and MD acknowledged that FY26 volumes were lower than expected. The company is shifting focus from internal combustion engines to EVs and plans to transition its entire vehicle portfolio to locally made, proprietary battery cells by September 2026.

Centrum Capital plans to raise up to Rs 1,000 crore via NCDs, alongside an additional Rs 1,000 crore via QIP or a rights issue.

John Cockerill India board is scheduled to meet on May 26 to evaluate fundraising options through equity and debt.

Rossari Biotech has completed the sale of its arm Unitop Chemicals' office property for Rs 10.5 crore.

India Glycols filed an appeal at the Delhi High Court against New India Assurance Co for the recovery of a Rs 79.7 crore insurance claim regarding damages at its Kashipur plant, which was previously rejected by the insurer.

Adani Power successfully completed the acquisition of a 24% stake in Jaiprakash Power Ventures, effectively taking over its 180 MW thermal power plant.

Mphasis has partnered with the Indian School of Business to establish a dedicated AI hub. The Mphasis F1 Foundation will invest Rs 20 crore over the next four years in Phase-1 of the project.

text:
Interesting divergence playing out globally

Markets linked to the AI trade are massively outperforming

AI linked Markets: South Korea, Taiwan, Japan

Domestic Growth / “Anti-AI” Markets: India, Indonesia, Philippines

Why this divergence?

Korea & Taiwan are directly benefiting from the AI infrastructure boom

Samsung & SK Hynix are riding the HBM memory cycle

TSMC is at the center of AI chip manufacturing

Japan is benefiting through semiconductor equipment & exporters

This is becoming a clear split globally:

AI Asia = Korea, Taiwan & Japan

Domestic Growth Asia = India, Indonesia & Philippines

Interesting part is that even within markets, the rally is becoming extremely concentrated

A handful of AI linked companies are driving entire indices higher

Atalanta Electricals' mgmt on overcapacity in Transformer space:

"Requirement is going to edge over the supply side for at least next 5 years for sure."

In this sort of situation, when prices correct due to temporary commodity issues affecting margins it might present a decent opportunity to add.

No recommendation

Carysil Ltd
 
Post earning - Management talk.

Revenue FY27 : 15-20% CAGR
Margin : 18-20%
Exports : 70%  ( Among this 50% currency hedge)

India business plan 

Target: ₹500 crore domestic revenue in <5 years.

~200 brand stores in India over the next 2–3 years on a franchise model to boost brand presence.

Entered modern trade (large-format retailers) across India after being previously absent.

Built a B2B vertical focusing on the top 100 builders and architects in India.

Launched an online brand “Carissa” and secured large deals with major e-commerce players.

Expect material India growth acceleration over the next 3 years.

Sentimentally Positive For SRF, Navin Fluorine, Stallion Fluoro

Trump eases refrigerant rule in a bid to address surging grocery costs

Something nobody's connecting: India's fertilizer production hit 62.37 lakh tonnes in just Mar-Apr 2026 while West Asia is in full crisis mode. Imports were only 15.39 lakh tonnes.

Five years ago we'd have been panicking. This time the plants kept running.

Quiet wins don't trend. But domestic urea capacity going from deficit to near self-sufficiency might be the most underrated industrial story of the decade.

Dalmia Bharat acquires select JP cement assets for ₹2,850 Cr (+₹550 Cr capex).

Deal includes: 5.2 mtpa cement & 3.3 mtpa clinker
Effective valuation: US$68/ton incl. capex.

Strategic good move for Dalmia 
✅ Expands Central India presence, closer to PAN-India scale
✅ Capacity to reach 66.7 mtpa by FY28 vs 75 mtpa target

Inline with Ambuja comments in past call when they hinted at recalibrating expansion & slowing capex push.

- Revenue increased 57% YoY to ₹165 Cr.

- Revenue increased 79% YoY to ₹553 Cr.
- PBT more than doubled during FY26.

- Vintage Coffee delivered ₹359 Cr revenue.

- Delecto Foods reported ₹89 Cr revenue.
- Subsidiaries driving consolidated profitability.

- Net worth increased to ₹569 Cr.
- Cash balance rose sharply during FY26.
- Operating cash flow turned positive.
- Borrowings increased alongside expansion activities.

Capital Allocation
- Equity raise stood at ₹196 Cr.
- Capex additions reached ₹157 Cr.
- Majority fresh capital deployed into expansion.

Key Watchpoints
- Receivables increased sharply during FY26.
- Working capital absorption remained elevated.
- Capex returns need monitoring ahead.
- Parent entity remains largely holding structure.

Final Takeaway
- Vintage Coffee delivered strong growth led by subsidiaries, expansion capex and improving operating cash generation.

GEM AROMATICS Q4 : ARM KRYSTAL INGREDIENTS : NEW PRODUCT COMMERCIALISATION & QUALITY AUDITS COMPLETED 🧪🌿

text:
U.S. ALLOWS SCRAP DEALER TO BUY SANCTIONED IRAN SHIPS

The U.S. has granted a license allowing a major ship scrap buyer to purchase four vessels sanctioned for carrying Iranian-linked cargo.

The move could open a legal exit route for parts of the “shadow fleet” used to transport sanctioned goods for Iran and Russia, which has expanded under Western sanctions.

The ships, tied to an Iran-linked network, will be dismantled for recycling. Officials say the case-by-case licensing approach may be used more broadly, helping remove high-risk vessels from global waters.

text:
DATAMATICS : BOARD APPROVES AMALGAMATION OF WHOLLY-OWNED SUBSIDIARIES 🔄💻

• 🏢 Board approved Scheme of Amalgamation of Dextara Digital Pvt Ltd (DDPL) and Datamatics Cloud Solutions Pvt Ltd (DCSPL) with Datamatics Global Services Ltd (DGSL)

• 🤝 DDPL and DCSPL are wholly-owned subsidiaries of DGSL

• 📅 Appointed date for amalgamation fixed as April 1, 2026

• 🚫 No new shares will be issued under the scheme as both entities are wholly-owned subsidiaries

• 📉 Entire paid-up capital of DDPL and DCSPL will stand cancelled upon effectiveness of scheme

• 📊 No change in shareholding pattern of DGSL pursuant to amalgamation

• 🤖 Merger aimed at integrating AI, cloud-based CRM platforms, Salesforce solutions & digital transformation offerings under one structure

• ⚙️ Company expects improved operational efficiencies, resource optimisation, cross-selling opportunities, simplified group structure & better cash management

• 🌍 DGSL operates across digital technologies, AI/ML, automation, intelligent document processing & business intelligence with presence across USA, India & Philippines

🟢 Impact: Positive (Internal restructuring expected to improve operational efficiency, strengthen digital capabilities and enhance long-term scalability)

DEE DEVELOPMENT ENGINEERS: STRONG GUIDANCE OVERSHADOWED Q4 PROFIT DECLINE

Q4 & FY27 Outlook
- Q4 revenue increased 26% YoY.
- Q4 net profit declined 11% YoY.
- FY27 revenue guidance set at ₹1,500 Cr.
- FY27 EBITDA margin guided above 19%.

Order Book & Inflows
- Current order book stands near ₹1,940 Cr.
- Export orders contribute over 60%.
- FY27 inflow target set at ₹2,000 Cr.
- Domestic-export mix targeted equally.

Margin Expansion Drivers
- Anjar plant now fully operational.
- Seamless plant ramp-up supporting margins.
- Operational leverage expected improving profitability.
- Pallet and power plants commissioned.

Thailand Subsidiary Update
- Signed agreement with major US EPC company.
- Around 60% capacity blocked till 2029.
- Includes idle-capacity protection clause.
- Revenue potential ₹120–130 Cr annually.

Anjar Pipe Plant
- Guidance maintained at ₹240 Cr revenue.
- Plant positioned as import substitute product.
- One major approval still pending.
- Utilization may exceed earlier expectations.

Project & Business Updates
- Dow Chemical Canada order continues execution.
- Annual escalation clause remains at 6%.
- Nuclear project timelines improving gradually.

Management Vision
- FY30 topline ambition near ₹2,500 Cr.

Final Takeaway
- Short-term profit weakness contrasted with strong execution visibility, margin expansion plans and long-term order pipeline strength.

text:
CITI on PI Ind
Sell, TP Rs 2600

At Rs3.37bn (below), PI’s 4Q EBITDA fell 26% yoy on lower CSM exports and domestic agchem revenues and lower EBITDA margin.

Pharma CDMO revenues grew 23% yoy (accounted for 7% of revenues in 4QFY26), although the segment continued to report EBITDA loss.

EBITDA was up 11% qoq, largely on seasonality.

EBITDA margin was 21.5% vs 22.0% in 3Q and 25.5% in 4QFY25.

Looking ahead while mgmt. expects revenue growth in FY27 – this is likely to be backended given relatively higher base in 1H.

With generic competition for PI’s largest molecule (pyroxasulfone) expected in US in CY26, muted outlook for the global agchem market & no immediate ramp-up of new segments
CITI Says Believe Al Theme Is In A Boom Rather Than A Bubble | We Are Underweight On Emerging Markets, But Overweight On Taiwan & South Korea

RATNAVEER PRECISION (+VE)

Maintain Buy, TP Rs 235-ICICI Direct (27% Upside Potential)

Bullishness From CCL Expansion

Jefferies on PI Ind
Buy, TP Rs 3575

Reported a miss on EBITDA and PAT.

New product growth has slowed, and pyroxasulfone is in sharp contraction.

Working capital rose sharply.
A favorable base and growth in new molecules should restore growth in FY27, with management expecting high-single-digit revenue growth

Forecast adjusted PAT Cagr of 12% over FY26-28E.

Jefferies on Jubilant Foodworks
Buy, TP cut to Rs 600 from Rs 850

With consumer tech platforms becoming preferred ways for investors to play consumption theme, JUBI's 4Q provided no reason to revisit name

Flat SSS & cautious ST margin commentary suggest wait for a turnaround continues.

Overall, 4Q beat modest JEFe.
Still, exit trajectory results in a 10-12% cut in EBITDA as mgmt aims for a calibrated price hike, which view as a prudent strategy

HSBC on Jubilant Foodworks
Downgrade to hold, TP cut to Rs 530 from Rs 530.

Like-for-like (LFL) of +0.2% had no one-off (LPG impact of 0.3-0.4%) and marked sharp moderation

1Q LFL looking marginally better but points to activation-led demand, making it a choice of growth or margins

Near-term inflation also a headwind; cut estimates

HSBC On PI Ind
Hold TP Rs 3000

4QFY26 earnings another miss given disappointment in Ag business; higher overheads pressure EBITDA margins

Zone of uncertainty: core businesses remain muted, and new businesses take time to scale up necessary investments
Cut earnings growth estimates

CLSA on Samvardhana Mortherson
O-P, TP Rs 173

4Q Ebitda margin came in at 11% (up 136bps QoQ) vs estimate of 9.7%.

Revenue grew 17% YoY, supported by strong growth in emerging businesses (aerospace +40% and consumer electronics 7.5x)

Believe a gradual ramp-up in the non-auto businesses along with rising focus in ASEAN markets will drive a 13% Cagr for organic growth over FY26-28CL.

Over & above this, there is scope for inorganic growth, considering a leverage ratio (net debt/Ebitda) at 0.5x (FY28CL) vs SAMIL being comfortable until 2.5x levels

Jefferies on Samvardhana Motherson
Buy, TP Rs 160

Expect 26% EPS CAGR over FY26-28.

Q4EBITDA grew 43% YoY, 19% above JEFe

While global auto volumes remain range-bound, SAMIL's recent acquisitions should boost growth in FY27-28; also like its expansion in electronics & aerospace

Higher commodity costs could pose some margin pressure in 1HFY27, but should get passed on to customers subsequently

Stock is at 26x FY27E PE vs last 5-year average of 22x.

CITI on Ola
Sell, TP Rs 26

4Q lower than estimates due to lower ASPs (partly due to accounting changes for extended warranty).

Gross margins are impressive, even adjusted for PLI.

Mgmt. is positive on EV demand, particularly in backdrop of rising fuel prices.

Ola’s market share is expected to improve as co focuses on enhancing its service offering.

1QFY27 guidance: 40-45k orders; Rs5-5.5bn revenue.

Mgmt. emphasized its efforts on cost reduction and cash flow generation (positive FCF for auto-business in 4Q).

While acknowledge Ola’s gross margin improvement, we’d look for sustained volume growth before getting more constructive
90% of Ola’s opex is fixed, & if volumes do not improve, EBITDA would be under pressure despite high gross margins

HSBC on OLA
Reduce, TP cut to Rs 33

Ola is focusing on sustainable volume growth & margin expansion after a year-long efficiency improvement programme

Cautious battery cell production scaling looks prudent, but delays have usurped a key competitive advantage from Ola

Given weak volume growth, cut est.

**BPCL – JEFFERIES VIEW**

- EBITDA beats estimates strongly
- Inventory gains support Q4 earnings
- Petrol/diesel marketing margins negative
- Recent fuel price hikes insufficient
- Higher crude premiums remain concern
- Freight cost pressure continues
- Rupee depreciation impacts profitability
- Longer shipping cycles raise working capital
- Net debt likely to increase
- Valuation attractive versus peers
- Trades at 1.1x forward P/B
- Strong 25% full-cycle ROE outlook

**Impact:** Attractive valuation despite near-term margin and cost pressures

**NOMURA ON BPCL**

- Maintains Buy rating on BPCL
- Target price set at ₹365
- Strong Q4 GRM aided profitability
- Inventory gains likely supported earnings
- Growth projects underway across business
- Capex intensity expected to stay high
- FY28 earnings recovery expected
- Trades at 5.9x FY28F EV/EBITDA

**Impact:** Long-term growth outlook remains positive

India Power Sector - key insights

India’s total installed capacity reached 533 GW in FY26

FY26 total capacity addition at 58 GW

Renewable generation grew 20%

Installed capacity mix
Thermal capacity at 249 GW
Solar capacity at 150 GW
Wind capacity at 56 GW
Hydro capacity at 51 GW
Nuclear capacity at 9 GW

Solar capacity has increased from 22 GW in FY18 to 150 GW in FY26

That is almost 7x growth in 8 years
FY30E at 288 GW

Cell manufacturing capacity is expected to rise from 25 GW in FY25 to 115 GW by FY30E

Ingot-wafer capacity is expected to rise from 2 GW in FY25 to 80 GW by FY30E

Polysilicon capacity is also expected to start scaling from FY27 onwards

Earlier, the opportunity was mainly in module assembly

Now the opportunity is expanding into cells, wafers, ingots & polysilicon

Gen Z's Sobriety Shift: A Global & Indian Reality 
 

Global alcohol giants have shed $830 billion in market value since 2021 (46% drop), driven by Gen Z drinking ~20% less than Millennials at the same age. Health awareness, wellness priorities, economic pressures, and alternatives like cannabis fuel this secular change.  
 
In India, the trend aligns with data: NFHS-5 shows declining alcohol use among men (29% to 19%), with even lower rates among youth. Strict laws, social norms, and rising fitness focus keep Gen Z consumption modest compared to Western peers. 
Industry response includes non-alcoholic innovation, but the consumer-led shift toward moderation appears structural, not cyclical. 
 
Bottom Line: This data-driven generational move signals long-term pressure on traditional alcohol markets, pushing adaptation toward healthier, premium, and experiential offerings worldwide—including India.

🧵 GS Global Market Views: Shortages drive the tape The Iran ceasefire compressed risk premia; shortages in commodities + AI supply chain are now the dominant capital flow story. After the sharp recovery, risks look more balanced  but volatility likely rises alongside further equity gains.

▪️ Iran re-escalation remains the key tail  deep downside still underpriced; hedges in European equities/credit/FX screen best

▪️ Macro optimism well priced  market-implied US growth at ~2.5%, already running ahead of GS's 2027 forecast

▪️ AI capex booming hyperscaler capex estimates revised sharply higher (2028 consensus: $815B → $919B); tech investment as % of GDP now above late-1990s peak

▪️ Value challenge intensifying  AI-related market cap gains continue to outpace estimated PDV of AI capital revenues; vulnerability to aggregation /extrapolation fallacies

▪️ Vol regime: high single-stock, low correlation  distributional vol (winners vs losers) outpacing macro vol; long-dated SPX vol still attractive

▪️ Rates running out of time for 2026 cuts  front-end hikes priced unlikely to be matched, but easing harder with inflation bulge + resilient labor market

▪️ FX: Dollar stable, deeper shifts beneath  sustained CNY appreciation thesis intact; NOK/SEK, AUD/NZD at multi-year extremes on terms-of-trade

▪️ EM carry still works  preferred longs: BRL, HUF, MXN, ZAR vs diversified funders (EUR, SEK, THB)

▪️ Core trade construct: stay constructive on risk + pair with long vol / downside hedges to navigate optimistic growth pricing, concentrated positioning, and long-end rate risk

Jefferies on Hindalco
Hold, TP Rs 890

Novelis' Mar-Q reported EBITDA was just $86mn (down 84% YoY); adjusted EBITDA, as per company, was $459mn, down 3% YoY but 17% above JEFe.

Rising gap between reported vs adjusted EBITDA due to potential insurance recovery from the fire incident is clouding underlying business performance.

Novelis expects the fire-impacted plant to restart in next few weeks.
Net debt rose 8% QoQ to $6.7bn

HSBC on Hindalco
Buy, TP Rs 1310

Novelis reported a strong operating performance in 4QFY26 with adjusted EBITDA/t at USD544

Oswego restart in next few weeks is ahead of schedule; Bay Minette remains on track and capex stays at USD5bn.

Expect underlying earnings to improve in FY27

**CITI on Hindalco
Neutral, TP Rs 1000
**
4Q adj EBITDA at $459m (7% ahead on insurance recovery) fell 3% yoy – tariff impact ($27m), Oswego ($53m, volume loss impact only); offset in part by better scrap spreads, cost efficiency and Sierre flood recoveries ($41m).

Volumes fell 12% yoy to 844kt – Oswego impact (~73kt) and softness in some specialties markets.

Adj EBITDA/t: $544 vs $430 in 3Q, $494 last year. Profitability was hurt further by $577m ($683/t) – below EBITDA due to Oswego fires.

Oswego will restart in a few weeks, ahead of end June26 timeline.

Novelis expects overall impact as: EBITDA $100-150m, negative cash flow $1.7bn ($1.3-1.6bn earlier), volumes 150-200kt.
Net debt was $6.7bn vs. $6.2bn in Dec25; net D/EBITDA 4.1x vs. 3.7x.

Mgmt is confident of achieving $600 EBITDA/t once Bay Minette commissions.

With Oswego restarting, Bay Minette nearing completion, strong business momentum, they expect to turn positive FCF by end FY27.

CLSA on Hindalco
O-P, TP Rs 1030

Adjusted earnings surprised positively, with a better FY27 outlook helped by resumption of Oswego hot mill, near-term stronger scrap spreads and benefits of cost takeout

4Q adjusted Ebitda/t of US$544 was above estimate, partly helped by stronger scrap spreads.

While scrap spreads could taper as Oswego restarts, reported profitability should see a sharp rebound in 2H

Novelis expects positive FCF generation from end-FY27 and hence leverage to taper down.

JPM on Hindalco
OW, TP Rs 1175

Novelis’ commentary ticked most of boxes as Oswego plant will restart slightly ahead of June end timeline & Bay Minette project cost/timeline remains on track

Management sees a strong order pipeline in automotive (doesn’t anticipate volume loss to competition) and highlighted that the pricing environment is positive in beverage can segment. 

Costs are largely well managed as Novelis is well hedged on energy, although scrap market may tighten once the Oswego/Bay Minette plants ramp up (not a major concern as MWP premium/scrap spreads are high)

While leverage ratios will deteriorate in near term (as expected), Novelis has guided to positive free cash flow in 4QFY27 with no further parental equity infusion

Believe investors will now monitor a) pace of the Oswego volume ramp up, b) confidence in Bay Minette driving incremental volumes in FY28, and c) scrap price trends.

**UNO MINDA – AXIS SECURITIES VIEW**

**VALUATION & RATING**
- Axis Securities maintains BUY rating
- Target price revised to ₹1,240
- Implies ~16% upside from CMP
- Valued at 36x FY28E EPS

**Q4FY26 PERFORMANCE**
- Revenue rose 17.8% YoY to ₹5,336 Cr
- EBITDA increased 14.5% YoY
- EBITDA margin stood at 11.3%
- Adjusted PAT grew 21.6% YoY
- Highest-ever quarterly profitability achieved

**BUSINESS MOMENTUM**
- Strong growth across all major segments
- Lighting, seating & EV segments performed well
- Green Mobility revenue rose 25% YoY
- Ramp-up seen in new manufacturing plants
- Premiumisation trends supporting growth

**CAPEX & EV STRATEGY**
- FY27 capex planned near ₹1,750 Cr
- New EV powertrain plant announced
- Strong order wins in lighting & infotainment
- Sunroof order book exceeds ₹350 Cr
- EV & hybrid transmission focus increasing

**FINANCIAL OUTLOOK**
- FY26–28 revenue CAGR estimated at 17%
- PAT CAGR projected around 28%
- ROE may improve to 23.2% by FY28
- Debt ratios expected to improve steadily

**RISKS**
- Delay in new plant ramp-up
- Slower EV adoption or premiumisation
- Macro and commodity-related uncertainties

**KALPATARU PROJECTS – SYSTEMATIX INVESTMENT VIEW**

**VALUATION & RATING**
- Systematix maintains BUY rating
- Target price fixed at ₹1,400
- Implies ~11% upside from CMP
- Valuation based on 16x FY28E EPS

**Q4FY26 PERFORMANCE**

- EBITDA increased 19% YoY
- EBITDA margin improved to 8.2%

- Lower debt supported profitability

**FY26 HIGHLIGHTS**
- FY26 revenue grew 22% YoY
- PAT increased 71% YoY
- Order book stood at ₹65,460 Cr
- L1 position around ₹3,200 Cr

**FY27 OUTLOOK**
- FY27 revenue growth guided above 15%
- PBT margin may improve 75–100 bps
- FY27 order inflow target above ₹30,000 Cr

**BALANCE SHEET & STRATEGY**
- Net debt reduced 53% YoY
- ROCE improved to 21.4%
- Strong T&D and B&F pipeline
- Middle East risks remain near-term monitorable

**DODLA DAIRY – SYSTEMATIX INVESTMENT VIEW**

- Systematix maintains BUY rating on Dodla Dairy
- Target price fixed at ₹1,335
- Implies ~27% upside from current levels
- FY26–28 revenue CAGR estimated at 13%
- PAT CAGR projected around 30%
- EBITDA margin may expand to 9.5% by FY28
- Africa business expected to sustain strong growth
- VAP contribution targeted at 32–34%
- FY27 margins may recover on price hikes
- Procurement costs expected to soften
- Maharashtra expansion plant targeted by FY27-end
- Uganda greenfield facility planned by FY29

**Impact:** Margin recovery & Africa expansion support long-term growth outlook

**Antique on **

CITI on ICICI BK
Buy, TP Rs 1720

Management meet Key takeaways

[1] Broad-based growth momentum led by step-up in retail (mortgages, PL, GL); corporate growth supported by a structural shift in funding from bond markets to banks; continued momentum in business banking.

[2] NIMs range-bound with mix optimization the lever, while CoD may remain elevated.

[3] West Asia crisis shows no visible portfolio stress; actively monitoring borrowers exposed to global trade and supply chain disruptions; tighter credit filters on new borrower onboarding in vulnerable sectors.

[4] Fee income trajectory likely to improve toward double-digit growth; opex discipline maintained with growth expected to be below revenue/asset growth.

[5] ECL transition - given existing buffers, no networth impact anticipated; credit cost to marginally rise on a flow basis.

CITI On Axis Bank
Buy, TP Rs 1620

Management meet takeaways

[1] West Asia crisis – No visible deterioration and remains comfortable on growth and asset quality; likely carries sufficient buffer to absorb most adverse scenario.

[2] Disciplined growth strategy: To outperform system average growth; critically growth will not come at the cost of risk discipline.

[3] Committed to through-the-cycle NIM of 3.8% given structural levers; we expect some near-term NIM pressure from the mix shift.
[4] ECL transition impact on networth to be marginal; structurally marginal increase in credit cost (sector-wide dynamic).

[5] Strategic deposit franchise deepening underway; comfortable operating at ~92% LDR +/- 2-3%.
[6] Likely to deliver positive operating leverage (on C/I basis) in FY27 and cost to assets will be optimized over medium term

text:
The Refining Liquidity Trap

OECD commercial diesel and gasoline inventories will collapse to a critical 20 days of demand by August and October if the Strait remains persistently shut without aggressive market solvers.  

Retail bulls are blind to the alpha trap in refining configuration. A 1pp rise in US diesel yield forcefully reduces finished gasoline yield by 0.8pp, turning product availability into a violent zero-sum game.  

This regime shift triggers a brutal bottleneck. While a $10 increase in the 3-2-1 refining margin historically boosts global utilization by 1.3pp, physical capacity limits and rigid price caps mean refiners cannot pass on soaring crude costs.  

Bottom Line:
Expect an aggressive drawdown in consumer discretionary margins. Avoiding an outright supply crunch requires a punishing 3% to 4% demand destruction driven entirely by surging pump prices.

Pricol limited 

SBI security bullish on pricol limited 

Upside ~33%

text:
MOTILAL OSWAL ON VODAFONE IDEA

Maintain Neutral with target price of ₹10 (earlier ₹9.5)

FY28E revenue and pre-Ind AS EBITDA estimates raised by ~3-6%, driven by ~2.5% higher ARPU

NUVAMA ON VODAFONE IDEA

Maintain Hold with a target price of ₹13.5 vs ₹10.5

Subscriber losses stabilize

Margin expands

Capex remains elevated

Uphill task to meet financial obligations

Sustainability of positive subscriber addition to watch out for

5G rollout expected to be slower as a weak balance sheet impairs VI's ability to invest in the network

Tweaked FY27E/28E EBITDA by -8.1%/-6.1%

EMKAY ON DEEPAK NITRITE

Upgraded to BUY from Reduce with target price of ₹2,000 vs ₹1,800 (upside ~15.2%)

Q4FY26 adjusted EBITDA rose ~98% YoY and ~46% QoQ, ahead of estimates

Al segment performance recovered, supported by steady domestic demand despite slower exports

Advanced intermediates segment expected to see margin recovery

Phenolics business delivered strong profitability on higher spreads

Phenol-2 capex remains on track, with revenue edu contribution expected from FY29

Company remains well placed on feedstock availability

Brokerage raised FY27/FY28 EBITDA estimates by ~12%/6% on improved pricing and volume growth

MS on OMCs

Diesel and Petrol saw another small price hike of 90paise/litre

Taking total price hike to $6.5/bbl (4-4.5% price hike) implying US$82/bbl Brent

Reduces transport fuel loss to $9/bbl for retailers

Expect further price hikes in coming weeks

Cooking gas losses of $1bn/month remain very high

Although the government has compensated for such losses in the past, as cooking gas is a controlled product

HPCL benefits the most, followed by BPCL

ELARA ON RATNAMANI METALS & TUBES

Maintain Buy

Target price: ₹3,249

Management guides for 31-37% revenue growth in FY27

Orderbook rises 13% YoY to ₹21.6 bn

Standalone Q4 revenue declines ~37% YoY amid export disruptions

Subsidiaries continue to perform well

Brokerage sees stronger order inflows from energy security and Jal Jeevan Mission push

Company remains net debt free

GOLDMAN SACHS ON POWER GRID

Maintain Buy

Target price: ₹380

Q4 PAT beats consensus estimates by ~16%

FY26 capex rises 53% YoY to ₹399 bn

FY26 capitalization at ₹293 bn exceeds guidance

Work-in-hand stands at ₹1.7 tn

Management guides FY27/FY28 capex at ₹370 bn/₹450 bn

Brokerage sees strong medium-term earnings growth from capitalization pickup

CLSA on Power Grid

O-P, TP Rs 324

PWGR’s capitalisation has shown a lofty pick-up, up 221% in FY26.

PWGR’s backlog slowed to +10% YoY with a loss of market share in India’s green corridors.

Its capex in FY26 rose 52% and it guided that it is on-track to achieve capitalisation growth of 14% over FY26-28.

Consolidated rec. FY26 PAT grew just 3% YoY on weak capitalisation in 1H and a shift towards tariff-based competitive bidding (TBCB) in capitalisation, while it kept its dividend flat YoY

Forecast regulated & competitive (RAC) equity Cagrs of 9% over FY25-30CL.

Jefferies on Power Grid

Buy,  TP Rs 340

March Qtr adj. PAT was in-line

Capex is up 53% YoY to Rs132bn and capitalisation is up 10x YoY to Rs153bn.

FY26 capitalisation at Rs282bn was up 3.1x YoY and exceeded the company’s upgraded guidance of Rs250bn.

Given this, raise earnings estimate for FY27E-28E by 3%.

Execution uptick gives confidence on PGCIL meeting its FY27E-28E capitalisation targets.

Nomura on Uno Minda

Buy, TP Rs 1494

4QFY26 EBITDA in line; rising EV adoption, premiumization and export push key medium-term growth catalysts

Management commentary: Uno Minda has gained market share across segments from both new and existing customers; plans to focus on higher value addition and execution in new segments; most orders announced have >2x asset turnover, providing growth visibility

Lower EBITDA estimates by 4%/0.3% for FY27F/28F to factor in near-term cost pressure & cut EPS by 6%/3% on higher investments

Stock is currently trading at 33x FY28F EPS of INR32, which believe is attractive

HSBC on Bharti Airtel

Buy, TP Rs 2400

Growth levers appear intact: rising mobile ARPU, expanding home broadband subs, rising FCF, and growth in dividends

Proposed stake increase in Airtel Africa would be EPS accretive and helps to recede promoter stake sale overhang

HSBC on Power Grid

Hold, TP Rs 295

PWGR beats capex and capitalisation guidance for FY26, guides for upcoming beat for its FY27 guidance as well

Also see new opportunities from intra-state transmission projects and BESS, which can increase TAM

JPM on Amber Ent

Neutral, TP cut to Rs 7650

3 main takeaways from earnings call,

1) Amber’s revenue growth guidance for FY27 came in below expectations; Consumer Durables growth at 12-13% (vs JPMe at 16%) inline with industry and Electronics at 40% (vs JPMe at 59%)

2) There have been some delays in bare PCB projects as trial production at Ascent Circuits will start from Sep-Oct’26 (vs earlier guidance of 2QFY27) and at Korea Circuits it expects construction to begin from Jun’26 and trial production by 3QFY28 (vs earlier guidance of 1HFY28),

3) Amber guided for EBITDA margins to compress by 50-100bps at a consolidated level in FY27 due to a combination of an increase in raw material costs (copper clad laminates and gold) and an increase in minimum wages in Haryana and Noida

Cut revenues by 7-8% and margins by 50-120bps, led by cuts in both Consumer durables and Electronics business, which drives 12-36% EPS cuts over FY27-28E
